title: A。なおB。
titleEn: Provides additional information; moreover, besides, in addition
explanation: Dùng để bổ sung thông tin sau nội dung chính, thường trong thông báo, văn bản trang trọng hoặc phần chú thích. Có thể hiểu là “ngoài ra”, “xin nói thêm”. Cấu trúc: A (sentence) + なお + B (sentence).
explanationEn: The なお grammar point is used to provide additional information or emphasize an existing fact. It can be translated as moreover, besides, or in addition in English. なお is placed between two sentences to connect them and provide supplementary information
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。もっとも B。
titleEn: Express contrasting opinions or facts; although, but, however
structure: Clause A + もっとも + Clause B
structureEn: Clause A + もっとも + Clause B
explanation: Dùng khi sau khi nói A, người nói bổ sung một điểm đối lập hoặc giới hạn ở B để điều chỉnh nhận định trước đó. Có thể hiểu là “tuy nhiên”, “dù vậy”. Cấu trúc: Clause A + もっとも + Clause B.
explanationEn: The A。もっとも B。 grammar point is used to express contrasting opinions or facts. It can be translated as although, but, or however in English. This structure connects two clauses and highlights the contrast between them
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: A。要するに B。
titleEn: Expressing in short, in a nutshell, or to sum up when restating or summarizing A as B
explanation: Dùng khi tóm tắt, rút gọn hoặc diễn đạt lại A thành kết luận B. Sắc thái “nói tóm lại”, “tóm lại là”. Cấu trúc: A。要するに B。.
explanationEn: The A。要するに B。grammar point is used to restate or summarize the information provided in A more concisely as B. It can be translated as in short, in a nutshell, or to sum up in English. The formation consists of two sentences or statements, A and B, with 要するに (yousuru ni) in between to indicate the summary or restatement of A as B
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun につき
titleEn: Indicates the reason, cause, or condition for something; due to, regarding, per
explanation: Dùng với danh từ trong văn trang trọng để nêu lý do, điều kiện, phạm vi liên quan hoặc đơn vị tính. Tùy ngữ cảnh có thể hiểu là “do”, “về”, “đối với”, “mỗi”. Cấu trúc: Noun + につき.
explanationEn: The につき grammar point is used to express the reason, cause, or condition for an event or situation. In English, it can be translated as due to, regarding, per, or for each. It can be used with nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun にて
titleEn: Expresses location or means; at, in, by, with
explanation: Dùng thay cho で trong văn viết hoặc ngữ cảnh trang trọng để chỉ nơi chốn, phương tiện, cách thức. Có thể hiểu là “tại”, “bằng”, “qua”. Cấu trúc: Noun + にて.
explanationEn: The にて grammar point is used to indicate the location, means, or method of an action. It can be translated as at, in, by, or with in English. It functions similarly to the particle で, but にて is more formal and often used in written language, formal speaking, and announcements
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun の ことだから
titleEn: Used to provide a reason or explanation based on ones knowledge about the noun; since, because, knowing
explanation: Dùng khi đưa ra nhận định hoặc lý do dựa trên hiểu biết về người/vật được nêu. Hàm ý “vì là người/vật như thế nên chắc là...”. Cấu trúc: Noun + のことだから.
explanationEn: The の ことだから (no koto dakara) grammar point is used when giving a reason or explanation based on one’s knowledge or understanding of a particular person or thing. It often implies given that this is the kind of person/thing it is
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を はじめ
titleEn: Meaning starting with, including, not only... but also
explanation: Dùng để nêu một ví dụ tiêu biểu rồi hàm ý còn nhiều đối tượng cùng nhóm. Có thể hiểu là “tiêu biểu là”, “bao gồm cả”, “bắt đầu từ”. Cấu trúc: Noun + をはじめ.
explanationEn: The grammar point Nounをはじめ is used to show that there are other things or people being included apart from the mentioned noun. It can be used to provide an example, indicate a representative or list items. This can be translated as starting with, including, or not only... but also... in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を はじめとして
titleEn: Expresses starting with, beginning with, or including
explanation: Dùng để nêu một đối tượng đại diện, rồi mở rộng sang các đối tượng liên quan khác. Sắc thái trang trọng hơn をはじめ. Có thể hiểu là “lấy ... làm đầu”, “bao gồm cả ...”. Cấu trúc: Noun + をはじめとして.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun を はじめとして is used to emphasize a particular item or example along with others. It translates to starting with, beginning with, or including in English. It is used to present a specific case and imply there are more related items
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
